response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong full-stack credentials, distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic (high-throughput, low-latency DLang services), and notable geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as the primary language, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014-2015) and no Rust. The geospatial background and low-latency systems experience are relevant, but the core language requirement is a significant gap that would require ramp-up.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map work
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems background at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Polyglot engineer comfortable picking the right tool and learning across the stack
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (one role, ~10 years ago) and no Rust
No demonstrated experience with routing tile pipelines or navigation guidance systems
Recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js, far from the required systems-programming focus
